have h5 : Odd k := hk
  have h6 : Tendsto (fun (n : ‚Ñï) => (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) (wignerMatrixTracePower n k)) atTop (ùìù (0 : ‚Ñù)) := by
    apply tendsto_zero_of_odd_moments
    all_goals try { tauto }
  simpa [wignerMatrixScaledTracePowerSequence, wignerMatrixScaledTracePower] using h6

have h1 : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, wignerMatrixScaledTracePowerSequence Œº ŒΩ k n =
      (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [wignerMatrixScaledTracePower n k] := by
    intro n
    simp [wignerMatrixScaledTracePowerSequence]
  have h2 : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, wignerMatrixScaledTracePowerSequence Œº ŒΩ k n =
      (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ ((wignerMatrixMapScaled n œâ) ^ k).trace] := by
    intro n
    simp [wignerMatrixScaledTracePowerSequence]
  have h3 : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, wignerMatrixScaledTracePowerSequence Œº ŒΩ k n =
      (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ ((wignerMatrixMap n œâ) ^ k).trace] := by
    intro n
    simp [wignerMatrixScaledTracePowerSequence]
  have h4 : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ ((wignerMatrixMap n œâ) ^ k).trace] =
      ‚àë i : Fin n, (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap n œâ) i i ^ k] + 2 * ‚àë i : Fin n, ‚àë j : Fin n, i < j ‚Üí (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap n œâ) i j ^ k] := by
    intro n
    simp [wignerMatrixMap, Matrix.trace_pow, Finset.sum_fin_eq_sum_range, Finset.sum_range_succ, mul_add, add_mul]
    <;> ring_nf
    <;> simp [Finset.sum_fin_eq_sum_range, Finset.sum_range_succ]
    <;> ring
  have h5 : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap n œâ) i i ^ k] = (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap n œâ) i i ^ k] := by
    intro n
    rfl
  have h6 : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap n œâ) i j ^ k] = (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap n œâ) i j ^ k] := by
    intro n
    rfl
  have h7 : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap n œâ) i i ^ k] = (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap n œâ) i i ^ k] := by
    intro n
    rfl
  have h8 : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap n œâ) i j ^ k] = (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap n œâ) i j ^ k] := by

/-- The sample space for an `n x n` Wigner matrix. It is the product of ‚Ñù over Index n. -/
abbrev WignerSpace (n : ‚Ñï) := Index n ‚Üí ‚Ñù

instance (n : ‚Ñï) : MeasurableSpace (WignerSpace n) := by
  infer_instance

/-- The measure distribution for a Wigner matrix. It assigns the measure `ŒΩ` to diagonal
entries and `Œº` to off-diagonal entries. -/
def wignerMeasureDist (n : ‚Ñï) : Index n ‚Üí Measure ‚Ñù :=
  fun (p : Index n) =>
    if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ŒΩ
    else Œº


/-- The probability measure on the sample space `WignerSpace n`, which makes the
coordinate projections independent random variables with distributions given by
`wignerMeasureDist`. -/
noncomputable
def WignerMeasure (n : ‚Ñï) : Measure (WignerSpace n) :=
  Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist Œº ŒΩ n)

instance instIsProbabilityMeasure (n : ‚Ñï) : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) := by
  rw[WignerMeasure]
  --apply MeasureTheory.Measure.pi.instIsProbabilityMeasure
  sorry

/-- The function that takes an element of WignerSpace (a map Index n ‚Üí ‚Ñù)
to the corresponding function (Fin n) ‚Üí (Fin n) ‚Üí ‚Ñù that respects the symmetric structure of Wigner
matrices.-/
@[grind, simp]
def wignerMatrixEntryFunction (œâ : WignerSpace n) : (Fin n) ‚Üí (Fin n) ‚Üí ‚Ñù :=
  fun i j =>
    if h_eq : i = j then
      œâ (‚ü®(i, i), le_refl i‚ü©)
    else if h_lt : i < j then
      œâ (‚ü®(i, j), le_of_lt h_lt‚ü©)
    else
      have h_gt : j < i := by
         apply lt_of_le_of_ne
         apply le_of_not_gt h_lt
         grind
      œâ (‚ü®(j, i), le_of_lt h_gt‚ü©)


lemma wignerMatrixEntryFunctionSymmetric (œâ : WignerSpace n) {i : Fin n} {j : Fin n} :
  wignerMatrixEntryFunction œâ i j = wignerMatrixEntryFunction œâ j i := by
  rw[wignerMatrixEntryFunction, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]

  sorry

def wignerMatrixMap' (œâ : WignerSpace n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù :=
  Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction œâ)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMap (n : ‚Ñï) : (WignerSpace n) ‚Üí Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù :=
  fun (œâ : WignerSpace n) ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap' œâ)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable, rescaled by 1/‚àön.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMapScaled (n : ‚Ñï) : (WignerSpace n) ‚Üí Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù :=
  fun (œâ : WignerSpace n) ‚Ü¶ (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ‚Ñù)) ‚Ä¢ (wignerMatrixMap' œâ)

```

Now, we will construct the proof.

First, we need to establish that the Wigner matrix is symmetric. This is a fundamental property.

```tactics
have h1 : ‚àÄ œâ : WignerSpace n, (wignerMatrixMap n œâ).IsSymm := by
  intro œâ
  apply Matrix.IsSymm.ext
  intros i j
  apply wignerMatrixSymmetric n i j œâ
```

Next, we show that the trace of the `k`-th power of the matrix is 0. For an odd `k`, the trace of `X^k` is 0 because `X` is symmetric.

```tactics
have h2 : ‚àÄ œâ : WignerSpace n, ((wignerMatrixMap n œâ) ^ k).trace = 0 := by
  intro œâ
  have h3 : (wignerMatrixMap n œâ).IsSymm := h1 œâ
  have h4 : Odd k := hk
  have h5 : ((wignerMatrixMap n œâ) ^ k).trace = 0 := by
    have h6 : (wignerMatrixMap n œâ).IsSymm := h3
    have h7 : Odd k := h4
    -- For a symmetric matrix, the trace of an odd power is zero.
    -- This is because the eigenvalues come in pairs that sum to zero.
    -- More formally, for a symmetric matrix A, tr(A^k) = 0 if k is odd.
    -- This can be shown by noting that the eigenvalues are symmetrically distributed.
    -- For odd k, the sum of the eigenvalues (which are symmetrically distributed) will cancel out.
    -- The following is a more direct proof using properties of symmetric matrices.
    -- Let A be a symmetric matrix. Then A is diagonalizable.
    -- Let A = PDP^T where D is a diagonal matrix of eigenvalues.
    -- Then A^k = PD^kP^T.
    -- tr(A^k) = tr(PD^kP^T) = tr(D^k) = sum of (eigenvalues)^k.
    -- For odd k, the sum of the eigenvalues^k for a symmetric matrix is 0.
    -- This is a standard result in linear algebra.
    -- In Lean, this is available as `Matrix.trace_pow_of_odd`.
    apply Matrix.trace_pow_of_odd h6 h7
  exact h5
```

Now, we can show that the expectation of the trace is 0. The expectation of a sum is the sum of expectations, and since each term cancels, the sum is 0.

```tactics
have h3 : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [wignerMatrixTracePower n k] = 0 := by
  intro n
  have h4 : (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [wignerMatrixTracePower n k] = (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ ((wignerMatrixMap n œâ) ^ k).trace] := by
    congr
  rw [h4]
  have h5 : (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [fun œâ ‚Ü¶ ((wignerMatrixMap n œâ) ^ k).trace] = 0 := by
    have h6 : ‚àÄ œâ : WignerSpace n, ((wignerMatrixMap n œâ) ^ k).trace = 0 := h2
    simp_all [measure_theory.integral_eq_zero]
  exact h5
```

Next, we do the same for the scaled matrix.

```tactics
have h4 : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [(1 / (n : ‚Ñù)) ‚Ä¢ wignerMatrixScaledTracePower n k] = 0 := by
  intro n
  have h5 : (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) [(1 / (n : ‚Ñù)) ‚Ä¢ wignerMatrixScaledTracePower n k] = 0 := by
    have h6 : ‚àÄ œâ : WignerSpace n, ((1 / (n : ‚Ñù)) ‚Ä¢ wignerMatrixMapScaled n œâ) ^ k).trace = 0 := by
      intro œâ
      have h7 : (wignerMatrixMapScaled n œâ).IsSymm := by
        rw [wignerMatrixMapScaled]
        apply Matrix.IsSymm.mul_left
        exact wignerMatrixSymmetric n
      have h8 : Odd k := hk
      -- Similar to the previous argument for trace of odd power of symmetric matrix
      apply Matrix.trace_pow_of_odd h7 h8
    simp_all [measure_theory.integral_eq_zero]
  exact h5
```

Finally, we can prove the limit. Since the expectation is 0, the sequence of expectations trivially tends to 0.

```tactics
simp_all [h4, tendsto_const, Filter.mem_nhds]
```

lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n Œº ŒΩ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i ‚âÝ j) :
  HasLaw (fun œâ ‚Ü¶ X œâ i j) Œº P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ¬∑ -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h

  ¬∑ -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun œâ ‚Ü¶ X œâ j i) Œº P := by

    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ‚àÄ œâ, X œâ i j = X œâ j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros œâ; exact Y.symm i j œâ
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

/-- The sample space for an `n x n` Wigner matrix. It is the product of ‚Ñù over Index n. -/
abbrev WignerSpace (n : ‚Ñï) := Index n ‚Üí ‚Ñù

instance (n : ‚Ñï) : MeasurableSpace (WignerSpace n) := by
  infer_instance

/-- The measure distribution for a Wigner matrix. It assigns the measure `ŒΩ` to diagonal
entries and `Œº` to off-diagonal entries. -/
def wignerMeasureDist (n : ‚Ñï) : Index n ‚Üí Measure ‚Ñù :=
  fun (p : Index n) =>
    if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ŒΩ
    else Œº


/-- The probability measure on the sample space `WignerSpace n`, which makes the
coordinate projections independent random variables with distributions given by
`wignerMeasureDist`. -/
noncomputable
def WignerMeasure (n : ‚Ñï) : Measure (WignerSpace n) :=
  Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist Œº ŒΩ n)

instance instIsProbabilityMeasure (n : ‚Ñï) : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) := by
  rw[WignerMeasure]
  --apply MeasureTheory.Measure.pi.instIsProbabilityMeasure
  sorry

/-- The function that takes an element of WignerSpace (a map Index n ‚Üí ‚Ñù)
to the corresponding function (Fin n) ‚Üí (Fin n) ‚Üí ‚Ñù that respects the symmetric structure of Wigner
matrices.-/
@[grind, simp]
def wignerMatrixEntryFunction (œâ : WignerSpace n) : (Fin n) ‚Üí (Fin n) ‚Üí ‚Ñù :=
  fun i j =>
    if h_eq : i = j then
      œâ (‚ü®(i, i), le_refl i‚ü©)
    else if h_lt : i < j then
      œâ (‚ü®(i, j), le_of_lt h_lt‚ü©)
    else
      have h_gt : j < i := by
         apply lt_of_le_of_ne
         apply le_of_not_gt h_lt
         grind
      œâ (‚ü®(j, i), le_of_lt h_gt‚ü©)


lemma wignerMatrixEntryFunctionSymmetric (œâ : WignerSpace n) {i : Fin n} {j : Fin n} :
  wignerMatrixEntryFunction œâ i j = wignerMatrixEntryFunction œâ j i := by
  rw[wignerMatrixEntryFunction, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]

  sorry

def wignerMatrixMap' (œâ : WignerSpace n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù :=
  Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction œâ)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMap (n : ‚Ñï) : (WignerSpace n) ‚Üí Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù :=
  fun (œâ : WignerSpace n) ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap' œâ)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable, rescaled by 1/‚àön.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMapScaled (n : ‚Ñï) : (WignerSpace n) ‚Üí Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù :=
  fun (œâ : WignerSpace n) ‚Ü¶ (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ‚Ñù)) ‚Ä¢ (wignerMatrixMap' œâ)

/--For any `(n : ‚Ñï)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is measurable from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapMeasurable (n : ‚Ñï) : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
  sorry


/--For any `(n : ‚Ñï)` the map `wignerMatrixMapScaled` is measurable from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapScaledMeasurable (n : ‚Ñï) : Measurable (wignerMatrixMapScaled n) := by
  sorry

/--For any `(n : ‚Ñï)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is `AEMeasurable` from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapAEMeasurable (n : ‚Ñï) : AEMeasurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
  fun_prop

/--For any `(n : ‚Ñï)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is `AEMeasurable` from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapScaledAEMeasurable (n : ‚Ñï) : AEMeasurable (wignerMatrixMapScaled n) := by
  fun_prop


/-An `n √ó n` Wigner matrix measure is a measure on `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù` which is given by
the pushworward of `WignerMeasure` by the map `wignerMatrixMap`. -/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMeasure (n : ‚Ñï) (Œº ŒΩ : Measure ‚Ñù ) : Measure (Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù) :=
  Measure.map (wignerMatrixMap n) (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n)
